text_excerpt: Contact us | Archbishop of York Skip to main content Archbishop of York Main navigation Search Close Menu Home The Archbishop About the Archbishop Ministry and role Meet Stephen Cottrell The Archbishop’s team Priorities About his priorities Faith in the North The North Prayer Evangelism News, speeches and sermons Bishopthorpe Palace About Bishopthorpe Palace History of Bishopthorpe Palace Seasons in the garden Prayer and worship Key links Safeguarding Contact us Other websites The Church of England Archbishop of Canterbury A Church Near You What are you looking for? Popular searches Faith in the North Office of the Archbishop of York Breadcrumb Home Contact us Back Contact us Use the contact form below to send a message to the office of the Archbishop. Alternatively, you can write to Office of the Archbishop of York, Bishopthorpe Palace, Bishopthorpe, York, YO23 2GE or call the switchboard during office hours (9am - 5pm) at 01904 707021. You may have a query which is answered on the FAQs . We value the letters and emails we receive, but due to the sheer volume, we are unable to read or reply to all of them. Safeguarding If you have a safeguarding concern, please contact Sukhbir Singh on 01904 707021 or Rob Marshall on 07914 668907. Media enquiries For media queries only, call 07738 354491. Name Email address Phone number (Optional) Subject Message I am happy to share my personal data with the office of the Archbishop of York in accordance with their privacy policy How to find us Our postal address is: The Most Reverend & Right Honourable Stephen Cottrell, Archbishop of York The Office of the Archbishop of York Bishopthorpe Palace Bishopthorpe York YO23 2GE To find us using what3words: ///dinner.dwell.author Prayer for the Day Sunday, 3 May 2026 The Fifth Sunday of Easter Your browser does not support the audio element. Risen Christ, your wounds declare your love for the world and the wonder of your risen life: give us compassion and courage to risk ourselves for those we serve, to the glory of God the Father. Amen. Download the app Safeguarding The Church of England is committed to safeguarding as an integral part of its ministry and mission, promoting a safer culture and the welfare of every child, young person and adult.
